chapter_outlines.title: Shattered Ink
chapter_outlines.chapter_goal: Shatter the crystal, initiate the living manuscript, and force the agents into the ultimate rite, raising stakes to instantaneous collapse.

planned_payoffs: The crystal shatters and reality rewrites itself
Elisa retrieves the void‑fragment from the Hall of Mirrors

outline_notes: Introduce void‑fragment as dark inked shard; its retrieval is a ritual of grief
Use metaphor density 0.65: words as ink, voices as splashes
Dialogue density 0.45: keep dialogue tight but consequential
Maintain close third person past tense
Ensure each agent’s signature appears as unique glyph, auto‑generated, visibly altering the Glass
End with the lattice pulsing, hinting at next cycle’s volatility
Mark Hall of Mirrors as collapsed artifact
Record Elisa’s sacrifice of mother’s memory
Log new glyphs on Glass of Memory after each signature
Price of the Glass of Memory.

must_fix: Show collapse tightening in real time—words solidifying, breath turning to ink, agents feeling the threat; replace generic lullaby with a vivid, sensory memory that physically tears from Elisa’s mind; reduce placeholder tags to only critical meta‑aware moments; give each supporting agent a distinct, costly reaction during the binding rite; integrate the Hall of Mirrors collapse into a single, vivid image and record it as a collapsed artifact.
